name: Onion-Cheddar Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut butter or margarine into small pieces.
2. In a medium bowl, combine flours and set aside.
3. Dissolve yeast in warm water. Add orange juice, water, butter/margarine, onion soup mix, sugar, salt, and 2 cups of the flour mixture.
4. Mix until smooth. Stir in enough of the remaining flour mixture until soft dough is formed and it pulls away from the sides of the bowl.
5. Turn dough onto a lightly floured board, then knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour. (Dough is ready if indentation remains when touched)
6. Preheat oven to 375°F. Punch down dough, then turn onto a lightly floured board.
7. Press into a 10 x 8-inch rectangle; spread with shredded cheddar cheese. Roll, starting at the 8-inch side, jelly-roll style; pinch ends to seal.
8. Place in a 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan, seam side down. Brush with melted butter, then top with remaining cheese.
9. Bake for 45 minutes or until bread sounds hollow when tapped. Remove and cool completely before slicing.
Makes 1 loaf.
